title: pouteria subcaerulea
intro: Pouteria subcaerulea, also known as the Brazilian cherry or guanandi tree, is a species of tree in the family Sapotaceae, native to South America. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark and leaves containing medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Pouteria subcaerulea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections due to its antimicrobial properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the active compounds with a solvent.
